title: Restore v69 features: user profiles, admin panel, YouTube connection, feedback button, trending, and messaging

requirements.text: Restore the user profile/account functionality: ensure ProfilePage displays the authenticated user's principal ID, display name, stats (total clips, average score, viral clips count), and a chronological clip activity list. Ensure ProfileSetup modal fires on first login to collect a display name. Ensure UserIdDisplay, UserRoleBadge, and related profile hooks (useQueries, useGetOwnRole) are wired up and visible in the UI.
acceptanceCriteria: Authenticated users can navigate to their profile page and see their display name, principal ID, and clip stats
ProfileSetup modal appears on first login for new users
UserRoleBadge and UserIdDisplay are rendered correctly in the profile and sidebar

requirements.text: Restore the full AdminPanel: ensure it renders collapsible sections for system controls (pause/resume/restart/shutdown via SystemControls), analytics (AppAnalytics with clip counts and upload bar chart), user management (UserStatusManagement for listing/searching/managing user roles and statuses), admin messaging (AdminMessaging to send messages to specific users), feedback submissions (FeedbackSubmissions list with delete), activity logs (ActivityLogTable), admin list/management (AdminManagement, AdminList), and the AdminDebugPanel. The panel must only be visible to owner-role users.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin panel is visible only to users with owner role
All collapsible sections (system controls, analytics, user management, messaging, feedback, activity logs) are present and functional
Admins can send messages to users, manage roles/statuses, delete feedback, and view activity logs

requirements.text: Restore the YouTube channel connection UI: ensure ChannelConnection component is displayed in the Scheduler page and/or sidebar, using the useYouTubeChannel hook to show connected or disconnected state. Ensure the Scheduler page renders the YouTube channel connection status alongside the scheduled uploads list and clip scheduling form.
acceptanceCriteria: ChannelConnection component is visible on the Scheduler page
Connected/disconnected state is shown based on useYouTubeChannel hook
Scheduler page allows selecting a clip and datetime and shows existing scheduled uploads

requirements.text: Restore the Report a Bug / Request a Feature button: ensure the FeedbackModal trigger button is present in both the SideNavigation (desktop) and BottomNavigation (mobile). The modal must allow users to choose between 'Bug Report' and 'Feature Request' types and submit a title and description via the useFeedbackSubmit hook.

quotes: the report a bug / request a feature button

acceptanceCriteria: A feedback button is visible in the sidebar on desktop and in the bottom navigation on mobile
Clicking it opens FeedbackModal with Bug Report and Feature Request options
Submissions are stored and visible in the admin panel's FeedbackSubmissions section

requirements.text: Restore the Trending Now section: ensure TrendingPage is accessible via navigation and renders the top trending clips sorted by viral/trending score, including the TrendingSidebar top-5 leaderboard and a responsive grid of ClipCards. Ensure the 'Trending' nav link appears in SideNavigation and BottomNavigation for all users (authenticated and anonymous).
acceptanceCriteria: Trending page is accessible from both desktop sidebar and mobile bottom nav
Top trending clips are displayed in ranked order with viral scores
TrendingSidebar top-5 leaderboard is visible on the trending page

requirements.text: Restore messaging functionality: ensure the MessagesPage is accessible via navigation for authenticated users, displaying the UserMessages component which lists the user's messages and allows viewing individual messages and composing replies. Ensure the 'Messages' nav item appears in SideNavigation and BottomNavigation for authenticated users. Ensure useSendAdminMessage and useMyMessages hooks are correctly wired to the message store.
acceptanceCriteria: Authenticated users can navigate to a Messages page from sidebar and bottom nav
Users can view received messages and send replies
Admin messaging from the admin panel correctly delivers messages visible in the user's message list

requirements.text: Restore the Social Feed: ensure SocialFeedPage is accessible via navigation, showing recent clip creation activity from all users with avatar initials, clip thumbnails, time ranges, and relative timestamps. Ensure the 'Feed' nav link appears in SideNavigation and BottomNavigation.

quotes: the stuff I had in version 69

acceptanceCriteria: Social feed page is accessible from navigation
Feed displays clip activity items with user avatars, thumbnails, and timestamps
Loading skeletons and empty state are shown appropriately

requirements.text: Ensure all navigation links — Clips, Trending, Feed, Gallery, Profile, Messages, Scheduler — are present and correctly routed in both SideNavigation (desktop) and BottomNavigation (mobile), filtered appropriately by authentication state. Confirm that all routes defined in App.tsx correspond to their respective page components.

quotes: Add back all of the past stuff

acceptanceCriteria: All main nav links are visible and working on both desktop and mobile
Auth-gated links (Gallery, Profile, Messages) only appear when the user is logged in
All routes resolve to the correct page components without 404 errors

constraints: Preserve the cyberpunk dark theme introduced in version 70 — do not revert the visual styling
Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
All Motoko logic must remain in the single backend/main.mo actor file
nonGoals: Reverting the cyberpunk UI redesign from version 70
Implementing real YouTube OAuth token exchange (stub/disconnected state is acceptable)
Fixing the preset buttons (Last 30s, Last 60s, Auto-Highlight) — that is a separate known issue
